threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still hesitate to order a flat white because you think it’s just coffee, not a ten-minute debate on oat vs almond milk.
Caught a guy singing opera on the 69 last night. Whole bus joined in, even the driver flashed his lights in rhythm. Only in Brixton.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw an old gentleman feeding stray cats outside the Arabic bakery at dawn, it hit me—this place has its own heartbeat.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a bar that serves homemade limoncello while hosting a punk show on a Tuesday. It's chaos, but it's ours.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by the way they casually ignore the Civic Center BART chaos, while a newcomer is panicking over the smoke and sirens.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy serenading the Muni crowd with a saxophone, just as the bus lurched to a stop. Whole bus erupted in applause, then he vanished into the fog.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Most tourists miss that Gràcia’s plazas are alive after 9 PM, filled with locals gathering for cheap wines and deep conversations about everything.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You'll see them trying to pay for everything in cash, clueless about the custom of using contactless cards on the metro. Classic rookie move.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think the bus is the quickest way to get anywhere; locals know the hidden shortcuts through the parks.
Heard a couple arguing on the N87 last night; she threw a half-eaten kebab at him. Classic Brixton Friday vibes.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I realized I was home? Hearing a street musician playing a remix of Turkish pop while the smell of döner wafted through the air on Karl-Marx-Straße.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg do you find a street vendor selling currywurst with vegan options while a drum circle grooves just a block away. That's Berlin for you.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: Only locals know that the smell of churros at 16th and Mission is a warning—locals are fueling up before the chaos of an overstuffed train.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 19 last week, driver stopped to help a guy untangle his shopping cart from the Muni wires. No rush, just a moment of humanity.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is all about the Gaudí hype; they miss the little plaça where locals gather to chat away the afternoon.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell a recent arrival by their baffled face when the tram loses power and everyone just shrugs it off like it’s normal.
